__data__.text: Public Works Commission Meeting 04-28-21 Item 4.A.

Public Works Commission Agenda Report

To: Chair Merrick and Members of the Public Works Commission Prepared by: Troy Spayd, Assistant Public Works Director/City Engineer Date prepared:   April 15, 2021 Meeting date: April 28, 2021 Subject: Capital Improvement Projects and Disaster Recovery Projects Status Report
RECOMMENDED ACTION: Receive and file report on the status of the City’s current and upcoming Capital Improvements Projects and Disaster Recovery Projects.

DISCUSSION:  Staff will provide a status update on the following active projects in the Fiscal Year 2020-2021 Capital Improvement Program:

Capital Improvement Projects (Design)

Marie Canyon Green Streets

¾

Updates:

  The  City  has  recently  received  Measure  W  funds  to  complete  this project.    Staff  is  working  on  the  project  plans  to  prepare  for  public bidding.

¾

Project Schedule:

 Complete Design: May 2021  Begin Construction: Winter 2021

PCH Median Improvements Project

¾

Updates:  Consultant is working on the final design. In March,

 the City be seeking proposals from consultants to perform construction management.

¾ Project Schedule:  Complete Design: Spring 2021  Begin Construction:

 Fall 2021

Agenda Item # 4.A.

PCH Signal Synchronization System Improvements Project

¾ Updates:

 65% design package was submitted to Caltrans in November 2020 and we are still awaiting comments to those plans.

 Consultant is working on final design documents.

¾ Project Schedule:

 Complete Final Design: May 2021

 Advertise: July 2021

 Award Contract and Begin Construction: September 2021

Civic Center Stormwater Diversion Structure

¾ Updates:

 A  Los  Angeles  County  Flood  Control  maintenance  agreement  is required for this project.

  Staff is waiting for the County’s approval of the agreement.

¾

Project Schedule:  Final Design: Completed.

Awaiting approved maintenance agreement from the County.

 Begin Construction: Estimated Summer 2021

Westward Beach Road Improvements Project

¾ Updates:

 The  project  requires  coordination  with  Los  Angeles  County  Beaches and Harbor.

  Staff is working out the final details of the project with the County and will be finalizing the design.

¾ Project Schedule:  Complete Design: Spring 2021

 Begin Construction: Fall 2021

Civic Center Water Treatment Facility Phase 2

¾ Updates:

 Project is at the 65% design phase.

 The  City  obtained  a  project  CDP  from  Planning  Commission  on February 1,

 2021.

 The  State  is  reviewing  the  City’s  application  for  the  State  Revolving Fund program.

 The project assessment engineer has begun the process of evaluating the project costs.

 Staff is rescheduling the community meeting to May 2021.

¾

Project Schedule:  Complete Design: December 2021

 Begin Construction: March 2022

Agenda Item # 4.A.

Malibu Park Drainage Improvements

¾ Updates:

 Staff  made  minor  modifications  to  the  project  plans  to  reduce  the overall project costs.

 Project is out to bid with bids due April 21, 2021.

¾ Project Schedule:

 Complete Design: March 2021  Begin Construction:

 June/July 2021

Annual Street Maintenance

¾ Project  Description:

    This  project  provides  for  the  reconstruction  and maintenance of City streets.

¾ Estimated Schedule:

 Complete Design: Spring 2021

 Begin Construction: Summer 2021

Capital Improvement Projects (Construction)

City Hall Roof Replacement

¾ Updates:

  City  staff  continues  to  review  project  related  material  submittals. Once submittals are approved, all materials will be ordered and shipped to the project site.

The roof ISO board membrane is on back order due to the recent storms in Texas which halted manufacturing. The estimated material arrival and start date is the middle of May.

¾ Project Schedule: May 2021 through July 2021

Bluffs Park Workout Station

¾ Updates:

  The  contractor  has  completed  and  installed  all  the  new  workout equipment and rubber surfacing. Remaining work includes punch list items only.

¾ Project Schedule: Estimated completion date is April 2021.

Civic Center Way Improvements

¾ Updates:

 Work  Hours:  Monday  through  Friday  7:

00AM  to  4:00PM,  Saturdays 7:00AM to 4:00PM

 Civic Center Way was reopened to the public on April 8th.

 While being open,  many  project  related  items  will  continue.

This  will  include intermittent traffic control (lane closures). The remaining items includes final  paving,  striping,  irrigation,  painting,  planting  material,  clean-up, and final detailing.

¾ Project Schedule: August 2020 through April 2021

Capital Improvement Projects (Not Started)

Bluffs Park Shade Structure

¾ Project Description:

  This project consists of the installation of four single-post shade structures at Malibu Bluffs Park

¾

Estimated Schedule:  Complete Design: Summer 2021  Begin Construction:

 Fall 2021

Vehicle Protection Devices

¾ Project Description:  Per Malibu Municipal Code Section 17.48.

070 requires vehicle impact protection devices to be installed for all parking spaces located adjacent  to  any  outdoor  pedestrian  seating  area.    There  are  two  locations within the City’s right-of-way  and City-owned  property that would require a vehicle impact protection device.

¾ Estimated Schedule:  Complete Design: Summer 2021  Begin Construction: Fall 2021

Malibu Road Slope Repairs

¾ Project Description:

  The existing slope adjacent to the beach access stairs at 24712 Malibu Road has been eroded and caused damage to Malibu Road.

¾

Estimated Schedule:

 Complete Design: Fall 2021  Begin Construction: Winter 2021

Birdview Avenue Improvements (CalOES Project)

¾ Updates:

 The  design  of  this  project  has  been  included  in  the  Malibu  Park Drainage  Improvements  project  and  updates  will  be  provided  under that project.

Outdoor Warning Sirens (FEMA Project)

¾ Updates:

  This project will be funded through a grant from FEMA after the Wooley Fire.  The project consists of hiring a consultant to develop a plan that includes  the  evaluation  of  a  siren  system  and  possible  locations.    Staff  is working on an RFQ to hire a consultant for the design.

¾ Project Schedule:

 Complete Design: Unknown

Disaster Projects (Construction)

None at this time

Disaster Projects (Completed) Guardrail Replacement Citywide (FEMA Project)

¾ Project  Description:

    This  project  consisted  of  replacing  the  damaged guardrail throughout the City as a result of the Woolsey Fire.

Corral Canyon Road Bridge Repairs (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project consisted of replacing fire damaged existing fencing and repairing the damaged embankment adjacent to the bridge.

Corral Canyon Culvert Repairs (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project consisted of replacing a portion of Corral Canyon Road that was damaged from a failed storm drain.Page 5 of 6
Agenda Item # 4.A.

Disaster Projects (Not Started)

Clover Heights Storm Drain (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project consists of design and construction of a few storm drains on Clover Heights.  The existing storm drain facility ends at the intersection  of  Clover  Heights  and  Harvester  Road.    During  storms,  this intersection floods and causes debris to block the road.  An extended storm drain towards the end of Clover Heights will help eliminate this issue.

¾ Estimated Schedule:

 Complete Design: Spring 2022

 Begin Construction: Summer 2022

Latigo Canyon Road Culvert Repairs (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project consists of repairing the existing storm drain on Latigo Canyon Road located approximately 2,500 feet from PCH that was damaged by the Woolsey Fire.

¾

Estimated Schedule:  Complete Design: Winter 2021

 Begin Construction: Spring 2022

Encinal Canyon Road Drainage Improvements (CalOES Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project consists of repairing damage storm drain facilities and roadway embankments that were damaged by the Woolsey Fire.

¾ Estimated Schedule:

 Complete Design: Winter 2021

 Begin Construction: Spring 2022

Storm Drain Master Plan (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project will be funded through a grant from FEMA after the Woolsey Fire.  The City will create a complete inventory of storm drains,  culverts,  debris  basins,  manholes,  and  other  drainage  structures within the City.

¾ Estimated Schedule:

 Completion Date:  Spring 2022
